title: Asolando - Prologue
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Poetן¿½s age is sad: for why?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In youth, the natural world could show
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ No common object but his eye
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At once involved with alien glow,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ His own soulן¿½s iris-bow.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And now a flower is just a flower:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Man, bird, beast are but beast, bird, man,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Simply themselves, uncinct by dower
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of dyes which, when lifeן¿½s day began,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Round each in glory ran.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Friend, did you need an optic glass,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Which were your choice? A lens to drape
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In ruby, emerald, chrysopras,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Each object, or reveal its shape
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Clear outlined, past escape,

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The naked very thing? so clear
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That, when you had the chance to gaze,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ You found its inmost self appear
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Through outer seeming, truth ablaze,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Not falsehoodן¿½s fancy-haze?

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How many a year, my Asolo,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Since, one step just from sea to land,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I found you, loved yet feared you so,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For natural objects seemed to stand
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Palpably fire-clothed! No.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ No mastery of mine oן¿½er these!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Terror with beauty, like the Bush
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Burning but unconsumed. Bend knees,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Drop eyes to earthward! Language? Tush!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Silence ן¿½tis awe decrees.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And now? The lambent flame is, where?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Lost from the naked world: earth, sky,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hill, vale, tree, flower, Italiaן¿½s rare
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oן¿½er-running beauty crowds the eye,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But flame? The Bush is bare.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Hill, vale, tree, flower, they stand distinct
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Nature to know and name. What then?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A Voice spoke thence which straight unlinked
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Fancy from fact: see, allן¿½s in ken:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Has once my eyelid winked?

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ No, for the purged ear apprehends
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Earthן¿½s import, not the eye late dazed.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The Voice said, ן¿½Call my works thy friends!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At Nature dost thou shrink amazed?
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ God is it who transcends.ן¿½

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Asolo: September 6, 1889.
meaning: About: Robert Browning was a British poet and playwright whose mastery of dramatic verse, especially dramatic monologues, made him one of the foremost Victorian poets.
